---
id: withdraw-signature-request
title: "Withdraw a signature request"
description: "Sometimes, signing a document may become unnecessary, or errors might occur"
sidebar_label: "Withdraw a signature request"
hide_title: true
hide_table_of_contents: true
api:

---

import MethodEndpoint from "@theme/ApiExplorer/MethodEndpoint";
import ParamsDetails from "@theme/ParamsDetails";
import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";
import StatusCodes from "@theme/StatusCodes";
import OperationTabs from "@theme/OperationTabs";
import TabItem from "@theme/TabItem";
import Heading from "@theme/Heading";
import Translate from "@docusaurus/Translate";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Withdraw a signature request"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"post"}
  path={"/signature-requests/{SR_ID}/withdraw"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



Sometimes, signing a document may become unnecessary, or errors might occur
during the invitation process. As the inviter of such a `SignatureRequest`,
you can _explicitely_ `withdraw` the `SignatureRequest`. Withdraw removes
all signatories and observers from the document as participants and notifies
them with a specified `message.`


Similar to `deletion`, the `withdraw` action can only be executed if:


- The SignatureRequest status is `status_overall=OPEN`

- The API user has sufficient rights on the `SignatureRequest`, such as when
it was created by the API user.
    

> **Note**  

We recommend using the `delete` endpoint. By default, it checks for other
options. For example, if the API user does not have withdrawal rights, the
user could be removed from the `SignatureRequest` directly, or the document
might be deleted directly.  

Nevertheless, there may be situations in which you need to withdraw the
document and keep it in Skribble. In this case you should of course use the
`withdraw` functionality.

<Heading
  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
>

</Heading>
